tools.name: codegraph_search
tools.description: Quick symbol search by name. Returns locations only (no code). Use codegraph_explore instead to get the actual source / understand an area in one call.

projectPath.description: Path to a different project with .codegraph/ initialized. If omitted, uses current project. Use this to query other codebases.

tools.name: codegraph_callers
tools.description: List functions that call <symbol>. For the full flow, use codegraph_explore.

symbol.description: Name of the function, method, or class to find callers for

limit.description: Maximum number of callers to return (default: 20)

projectPath.description: Path to a different project with .codegraph/ initialized. If omitted, uses current project. Use this to query other codebases.

tools.name: codegraph_callees
tools.description: List functions that <symbol> calls. For the full flow, use codegraph_explore.

symbol.description: Name of the function, method, or class to find callees for

limit.description: Maximum number of callees to return (default: 20)

projectPath.description: Path to a different project with .codegraph/ initialized. If omitted, uses current project. Use this to query other codebases.

tools.name: codegraph_impact
tools.description: List symbols affected by changing <symbol>. Use before a refactor.

symbol.description: Name of the symbol to analyze impact for

depth.description: How many levels of dependencies to traverse (default: 2)

projectPath.description: Path to a different project with .codegraph/ initialized. If omitted, uses current project. Use this to query other codebases.

tools.name: codegraph_node
tools.description: SECONDARY (after codegraph_explore): get ONE symbol in full — its location, signature, callers/callees trail, and verbatim body (includeCode=true). When the name is AMBIGUOUS (an overloaded method, or the same method name on different types), it returns EVERY matching definition's full body in a single call — so you never need to Read a file to find the specific overload you want. For a heavily-overloaded name, pass `file` (and/or `line`) to pin the exact definition — e.g. the `file:line` a trail or another tool already showed you. Reach for this when explore trimmed a body you need. Use codegraph_explore for several related symbols or the full flow.

symbol.description: Name of the symbol to get details for

includeCode.description: Include full source code (default: false to minimize context)

file.description: Optional: disambiguate an overloaded name to the definition in this file (path or basename, e.g. "harness.rs").

line.description: Optional: disambiguate to the definition at/around this line (use with the file:line a trail showed you).

projectPath.description: Path to a different project with .codegraph/ initialized. If omitted, uses current project. Use this to query other codebases.

tools.name: codegraph_explore
tools.description: PRIMARY TOOL — call FIRST for almost any question: how does X work, architecture, a bug, where/what is X, or surveying an area. Returns the verbatim source of the relevant symbols grouped by file in ONE capped call (Read-equivalent — do NOT re-open shown files). Query can be a natural-language question OR a bag of symbol/file names. Usually the ONLY call you need — answers without further search/node/Read/Grep.

query.description: Symbol names, file names, or short code terms to explore (e.g., "AuthService loginUser session-manager", "GraphTraverser BFS impact traversal.ts"). Use codegraph_search first to find relevant names.

maxFiles.description: Maximum number of files to include source code from (default: 12)

projectPath.description: Path to a different project with .codegraph/ initialized. If omitted, uses current project. Use this to query other codebases.

tools.name: codegraph_status
tools.description: Index health check (files / nodes / edges). Skip unless debugging.

projectPath.description: Path to a different project with .codegraph/ initialized. If omitted, uses current project. Use this to query other codebases.

tools.name: codegraph_files
tools.description: Indexed file tree with language + symbol counts. Faster than Glob for project layout.

path.description: Filter to files under this directory path (e.g., "src/components"). Returns all files if not specified.

pattern.description: Filter files matching this glob pattern (e.g., "*.tsx", "**/*.test.ts")

includeMetadata.description: Include file metadata like language and symbol count (default: true)

maxDepth.description: Maximum directory depth to show (default: unlimited)

projectPath.description: Path to a different project with .codegraph/ initialized. If omitted, uses current project. Use this to query other codebases.
